page: Amazon Web Services i8g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom ARM64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 9 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong memory and decompression capabilities. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score and strong ratings in large-block memory bandwidth, with read speeds of 296.95 GB/sec and write speeds of 130.36 GB/sec. Single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores are also strong, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 202.88 GB/sec. However, LLM inference performance is a notable tradeoff, showing weak to poor results, particularly for text generation on larger models, such as 2.29 tokens/sec on a 70B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of high-capacity local NVMe storage with a memory-dense ARM64 architecture offers a balanced resource profile for data-intensive operations without the overhead of external storage attachments. This makes the instance suitable for database operations, as reflected in its strong PassMark database benchmark of 24105.77 operations. Appropriate workloads include local database hosting, caching layers like Redis, data decompression pipelines, and general-purpose storage-heavy applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance built on the ARM64 architecture using the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an AWS Neoverse-V2 processor clocked at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD. The instance delivers strong memory performance, highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong large-block memory bandwidth of 296.95 GB/sec read and 130.36 GB/sec write. While CPU and decompression benchmarks are strong, LLM inference speeds represent a performance tradeoff, with poor throughput on larger models. Qualitatively, the combination of high-density local NVMe storage and 8.0 GB of RAM per core provides a cost-effective platform for storage-heavy workloads. It is best suited for database operations, caching systems, and data decompression pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 3750 GB local NVMe SSD for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.4xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server with 16 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 3750 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized platform featuring dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, high-density local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier PassMark memory score and strong large-block read/write bandwidth
Suited for database operations, caching, and data decompression
